heading: Vessel deemed public vessel
label: 3908 Vessel deemed public vessel
text: While assigned to authorized Coast Guard duty, any motorboat or yacht shall be deemed to be a public vessel of the United States and a vessel of the Coast Guard within the meaning of sections 937 and 938 of this title and other applicable provisions of law.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 14, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 266 (, title I, § 7, ).Feb. 19, 1941, ch. 855 Stat. 10Changes were made in phraseology. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.

Amendments2018—, substituted “sections 937 and 938” for “sections 646 and 647”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2), renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 119(b)section 827 of this title1996— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any motorboat or yacht, while assigned to authorized Coast Guard duty shall be deemed to be a public vessel of the United States, and within the meaning of shall be deemed to be a vessel of the Coast Guard.”Pub. L. 104–324section 646 of this title
